zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan de la Pole於大約1355年出生在英國艾可可利沙拉爾時，她的父親John de la Pole16歲，母親Joan Cobham15歲。她於1380年11月之前與Robert Hemenhale結婚。她去世於1434年1月13日在英國肯特可巴姆享年80歲，埋葬在英國肯特可巴姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan de la Pole于大约1355年出生在英国艾可可利沙拉爾时，她的父亲John de la Pole16岁，母亲Joan Cobham15岁。她于1380年11月之前与Robert Hemenhale结婚。她去世于1434年1月13日在英国肯特可巴姆享年80岁，埋葬在英国肯特可巴姆。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Pole
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Leicestershire): variant of   Paul   or   Poole  .
Americanized form of German   Pohle   or   Pohl  .

variants.name: Poles
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Livio, Primo, Santo.
English and Welsh: variant of Pole and Powell + genitival or excrescent -s.
Italian (Friuli and Veneto): probably a derivative of the personal name   Polo  . It is also found in Slovenia.

variants.name: Paul
meanings: English, French, German, Dutch, Swedish, West Indian (mainly Haiti, also e.g. Saint Lucia), and African (mainly Nigeria and Tanzania): from the personal name Paul (from Latin Paulus ‘small’), which has always been popular in Christendom. It was the name adopted by the Pharisee Saul of Tarsus after his conversion to Christianity on the road to Damascus in about AD 34. He was a most energetic missionary to the Gentiles in the Roman Empire, and played a very significant role in establishing Christianity as a major world religion. The name was borne also by numerous other early Christian saints. It is also occasionally borne by Jews; the reasons for this are not clear. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages and their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Greek Pavlis , Slovenian Pavel and Pavlič (see Pavlic ), Polish Paweł (see Pawel ) and Pawlicki , Assyrian/Chaldean Polous and Polus . In France, this surname is most common in Brittany (see 2 below).
Breton (mainly Finistère): from a Frenchified form of the personal name Paol, Breton form of Paul .
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Phóil ‘son of Paul’. Compare McFall .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan de la Pole was born about 1355, in Chrishall, Essex, England, her father, John de la Pole, was 16 and her mother, Joan Cobham, was 15. She married Robert Hemenhale before November 1380. She died on 13 January 1434, in Cobham, Kent, England, at the age of 80, and was buried in Cobham, Kent, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan de la Pole est née environ 1355, à Chrishall, Essex, Angleterre, son père, John de la Pole, avait 16 ans et sa mère, Joan Cobham, avait 15 ans. Elle a épousé Robert Hemenhale avant novembre 1380. Elle est décédée le 13 janvier 1434, à Cobham, Kent, Angleterre, à l’âge de 80 ans, et a été enterrée à Cobham, Kent, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan de la Pole um 1355 in Chrishall, Essex, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John de la Pole 16 und ihre Mutter Joan Cobham 15. Sie hat vor November 1380 Robert Hemenhale geheiratet. Sie starb am 13. Januar 1434 im Alter von 80 Jahren in Cobham, Kent, England und wurde in Cobham, Kent, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de la Pole è nata il circa 1355 nel Chrishall, Essex, England, sua padre, John de la Pole, aveva 16 anni e sua madre, Joan Cobham, aveva 15 anni. Lei sposato Robert Hemenhale prima del novembre 1380. Morì il 13 gennaio 1434, nel Cobham, Kent, England, all'età di 80 anni, e fu sepolta a Cobham, Kent, England.

ja.Chrishall, Essex, England: イギリスエセックスチリシャル
ja.Cobham, Kent, England: イギリスケントコブハム
ja.lifeSummary: Joan de la Poleが約1355年にイギリスエセックスチリシャルで生まれた時，父John de la Poleは16歳，母Joan Cobhamは15歳であった。1380年11月以前にRobert Hemenhaleと結婚した。彼女は1434年1月13日にイギリスケントコブハムで80歳で死去し，イギリスケントコブハムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan de la Pole(이)가 대략 1355년 영국 에세스 크리샤에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John de la Pole(은)는 16세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan Cobham(은)는 15세였다. 그녀는 1380년 11월 이전 Robert Hemenhale(와)과 결혼했다. 그녀는 1434년 1월 13일 영국 켄트 컵함에서 80세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 켄트 컵함에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de la Pole nasceu aproximadamente 1355, em Chrishall, Essex, Inglaterra, seu pai, John de la Pole, tinha 16 anos e sua mãe, Joan Cobham, tinha 15 anos. Ela casou-se com Robert Hemenhale antes novembro de 1380. Ela faleceu em 13 de janeiro de 1434, em Cobham, Kent, Inglaterra, com 80 anos, e foi sepultada em Cobham, Kent, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan de la Pole родилась около 1355 г. в местности кришолл, эссекс, Англия, её отцу по имени John de la Pole было 16 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan Cobham, 15. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert Hemenhale перед ноябрь 1380 г. Она умерла 13 января 1434 г. в местности кобхам, кэнт, Англия в возрасте 80 лет и была похоронена в местности кобхам, кэнт, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan de la Pole nació aproximadamente 1355, en Chrishall, Essex, Inglaterra, su papá, John de la Pole, tenía 16 años y su mamá, Joan Cobham, tenía 15 años. Se casó con Robert Hemenhale antes de noviembre de 1380. Murió el 13 de enero de 1434, en Cobham, Kent, Inglaterra, a la edad de 80 años, y fue sepultada en Cobham, Kent, Inglaterra.
